description:
This composite image presents three distinct choropleth maps of Europe, illustrating different aspects of land cover and land use.

The top map displays broad biogeographical regions or ecological zones across Europe, using a variety of colours. Dark blue represents northernmost regions (e.g., Iceland, northern Scandinavia). Shades of light blue cover the British Isles, parts of the Baltic Sea region, and western France. Various greens dominate Central Europe, Sweden, and Finland. Purple areas are visible in the Alpine region. Light brown, dark brown, and pinkish-brown colours depict regions in the Mediterranean and Eastern Europe. Small yellow features are visible in the Atlantic Ocean, likely representing islands.

The middle map illustrates a highly detailed and fragmented distribution of diverse land cover or land use types across Europe. The map is coloured with a dominant mix of pink/red, multiple shades of green, yellow/orange, and some grey/purple, reflecting a granular and varied landscape, consistent with a detailed land cover classification or a "Crop Mask" derived from products like VLCC CTY.

The bottom map visualizes the distribution of urban areas across Europe, functioning as an "Urban Mask". The map shows a grey background with numerous distinct yellow/orange patches, which represent urban areas. These urban areas are particularly concentrated in Western and Central Europe, along major river valleys, and coastal regions, extending into parts of Eastern Europe. This spatial pattern is indicative of urban land use, likely derived from an Urban Atlas (UA) product.

No legends, scale bars, or specific reference years are visible on any of the maps.
